learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate basic fingerstyle guitar technique and performance skills
Understand foundational musicianship through note reading and rhythmic skills, and perform with appropriate expression and style
Demonstrate a capacity for reflection and problem solving in your musical practice
Demonstrate an ability to work in small ensemble settings to create a cohesive performance

assessments.due_date:
Checkpoint 1 In-Person Performance will occur in class during Week 4 17/08/2026 - 21/08/2026

Checkpoint 2 Video Performance - Due 5:00pm 18/09/2026 - 18/09/2026
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is a “checkpoint” that ensures you have the necessary skills to perform to an appropriate standard for the Practical Assessment performance, and provides a formal opportunity for feedback. The lecturer will discuss in class the skills and repertoire that you will need to demonstrate, and provide feedback based on the assessment criteria for the Practical Assessment performance. 

Checkpoint 1 will take place as a performance during class time in Week 4. 

Checkpoint 2 provides an opportunity to practice recording a video in the style that you will submit for the Practical Assessment performance. Video submissions for Checkpoint 2 must adhere to the following requirements: 

  • Each performance is to be one continuous take. 
  • Your hands, fretboard and face must be visible. 
  • Students should announce their name, the date of recording. and the title of the work performed. For example, “My name is Jane Smith. The date is 18 September 2025 and my first piece is xxxxxx.” 
  • A high-quality recording device should be used, i.e. a recent model smart phone (using the back camera) or tablet might be suitable. Whatever device you choose to use to record your video exam, please ensure you test it first for both audio and visual quality. 
  •  Video resolution of 720p is recommended (minimum 480p). 
  •  Choose a quiet location with good lighting and minimal ambient noise. 


This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Practical Assessment on Guitar with Practice Journal
assessments.task_description:
Part A (30%): Video Performance

You will select five pieces to perform, demonstrating a range of styles that you will have learned and studied in class. The tutor will list which pieces you may choose from the material given out in class. 

Methods of performing these will be discussed in detail in class. 

  • Two classical pieces must be taken from the Classical Repertoire list detailed on Blackboard. These pieces will be handed out and discussed in class. 
  • Two pieces must be taken from the Popular Repertoire list detailed on Blackboard and handed out and discussed in class. 
  • The fifth piece may be taken from either the Classical or Popular Repertoire lists. No preference is given to either style.  


Each student is expected to display competency with a variety of techniques and perform with appropriate musical expression and style, as discussed in class. Each student should also demonstrate their understanding of the technique and posture practised in class throughout the semester. 

Practical Assessment video submissions must adhere to the following requirements: 

  • Each performance is to be one continuous take. 
  • Your hands, fretboard and face must be visible. 
  • Students should announce their name, the date of recording. and the title of the work performed. For example, “My name is Jane Smith. The date is 18 September 2025 and my first piece is xxxxxx.” 
  • A high-quality recording device should be used, i.e. a recent model smart phone (using the back camera) or tablet might be suitable. Whatever device you choose to use to record your video exam, please ensure you test it first for both audio and visual quality. 
  •  Video resolution of 720p is recommended (minimum 480p). 
  •  Choose a quiet location with good lighting and minimal ambient noise. 


Part B (20%): Weekly Practice Journal

Along with your video performance, you will submit a weekly practice journal showing thoughtful reflections on your learnings, progress and practice strategies each week of the course. This task shows how you find the links between your personal experience learning music and your learnings throughout course. Advice on how to complete this will be discussed in tutorials and available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In pairs, you will select one piece to perform together as a duet, demonstrating melodic and accompaniment skills in a cohesive small ensemble performance. The tutor will list the pieces you may choose from the material given out in class. You will rehearse your pieces together during and outside of class time. You will video record your performance and upload to Blackboard. 

You will also be required to complete a peer evaluation via Buddycheck to reflect on the rehearsal process and your group’s performance. The evaluation will represent a portion of your mark for this course (5%). 

Video submissions must adhere to the following requirements: 

  • Each performance is to be one continuous take. 
  • Both performers and their hands, fretboards and faces must be visible. 
  • Students should announce their names, the date of recording. and the title of the work performed. For example, “My name is Jane Smith. The date is 18 September 2025 we are performing xxxxxx.” 
  • A high-quality recording device should be used, i.e. a recent model smart phone (using the back camera) or tablet might be suitable. Whatever device you choose to use to record your video exam, please ensure you test it first for both audio and visual quality. 
  •  Video resolution of 720p is recommended (minimum 480p). 
  •  Choose a quiet location with good lighting and minimal ambient noise.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Weekly Tutorials

The course is delivered in two one-hour tutorials per week; students are encouraged to attend and participate in both tutorials.

Learning activities will be focused on the practical study of the guitar, in a range of different styles. This will include topics such as:

General guitar care

General music terminology

Location of notes

Correct technique

Context of guitar history

learning_activities.topic:
Personal Practice

Students are expected to spend approximately 8 hours per week in individual practice and preparation time.
